name: Madridejos, Puerto Lápice, and Villafranca Lagoons Loop
name_original: Camuñas, Villafranca de los Caballeros y Puerto Lápice – circular desde Madridejos
description: Cycle 37.7 miles (60.7 km) on this moderate touring route from Madridejos, visiting Laguna Grande and historic windmills.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Madridejos, located in the Castilla-La Mancha region of Spain, offers routes across the plains of La Mancha. The landscape is characterized by vast open fields, agricultural lands, and rural scenery. Cyclists can encounter the iconic windmills associated with the Route of Don Quixote, providing a cultural backdrop to the physical activity. The region's terrain generally features gentle ascents and descents suitable for touring. Best touring cycling routes around Madridejos The most popular touring cycling route is Camuñas, Villafranca de los Caballeros y Puerto Lápice – circular from Madridejos, a 37.7 miles (60.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 13 minutes to complete. This moderate route traverses open plains and connects several towns. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is El Castillo y Los Molinos de Consuegra – circular passing through Madridejos and Villafranca de los Caballeros, a difficult 42.9 miles (69.0 km) path. This route features views of historical castles and the famous windmills of Consuegra. Local touring cyclists also love the The castle and mills of Consuegra to Madridejos – circular passing through Villacañas, a 42.1 miles (67.8 km) trail leading through rural landscapes and past cultural landmarks, often completed in about 4 hours 54 minutes. Touring cycling around Madridejos is defined by vast agricultural plains, iconic windmills, and historical towns. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging routes. The routes in Madridejos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from a few reviews. More than 190 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Madridejos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Madridejos?
A: There are over 40 touring cycling routes around Madridejos, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. The komoot community has explored these routes over 190 times.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Madridejos?
A: The spring and autumn seasons are ideal for touring cycling in Madridejos. During these times, temperatures are more favorable, allowing for comfortable rides across the plains of La Mancha. Summers can be very hot, and winters may bring snow, making cycling less enjoyable.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Madridejos?
A: The terrain around Madridejos is primarily characterized by the plains of La Mancha, featuring vast open fields and agricultural lands. Routes generally include gentle ascents and descents, making them suitable for touring cyclists. You'll often find yourself cycling through rural scenery with a historical and cultural backdrop.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Madridejos?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Madridejos are circular. For example, the moderate route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022701657/camunas-villafranca-de-los-caballeros-y-puerto-lapice-circular-from-madridejos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camuñas, Villafranca de los Caballeros y Puerto Lápice – circular from Madridejos&lt;/a&gt; is a 37.7-mile (60.7 km) loop that connects several towns. Another popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022701446/the-castle-and-mills-of-consuegra-to-madridejos-circular-passing-through-villacanas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The castle and mills of Consuegra to Madridejos – circular passing through Villacañas&lt;/a&gt;, a 42.1-mile (67.8 km) trail through rural landscapes.

Q: What historical landmarks or natural features can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Touring cycling routes around Madridejos often pass by iconic windmills and historical sites. You can encounter the famous windmills of Consuegra, which are closely associated with Don Quixote. Other notable points of interest include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2654903" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Consuegra Castle&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5020745" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto Lápice Windmills&lt;/a&gt;. Natural features like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3902682" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calderico Hill&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5421216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Grande de Villafranca&lt;/a&gt; also offer scenic views.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Madridejos offers routes for experienced touring cyclists. For instance, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022588071/el-castillo-y-los-molinos-de-consuegra-circular-passing-through-madridejos-and-villafranca-de-los-caballeros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Castillo y Los Molinos de Consuegra – circular passing through Madridejos and Villafranca de los Caballeros&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 42.9-mile (69.0 km) path featuring significant elevation changes and views of historical castles and windmills. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20134351" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Consuegra Windmills – Consuegra Castle loop from Consuegra&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 30.6 miles (49.3 km) with over 560 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Madridejos?
A: The touring cycling routes in Madridejos are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 5.0 stars from several reviews. Cyclists often praise the unique experience of pedaling among the iconic windmills and exploring the historical and cultural sites that inspired Cervantes' Don Quixote.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in the area?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations are not provided, the region's generally gentle terrain and numerous moderate routes make many options suitable for families. Routes that focus on flat agricultural plains and connect charming towns would be good choices for a family outing, allowing for stops at cultural landmarks.

Q: What is the typical duration for a touring cycling route around Madridejos?
A: The duration of touring cycling routes around Madridejos varies depending on the distance and difficulty. Many popular routes, such as the 37.7-mile (60.7 km) &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022701657/camunas-villafranca-de-los-caballeros-y-puerto-lapice-circular-from-madridejos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camuñas, Villafranca de los Caballeros y Puerto Lápice – circular from Madridejos&lt;/a&gt;, can be completed in around 4 hours. Longer and more challenging routes, like the 42.9-mile (69.0 km) &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022588071/el-castillo-y-los-molinos-de-consuegra-circular-passing-through-madridejos-and-villafranca-de-los-caballeros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Castillo y Los Molinos de Consuegra – circular passing through Madridejos and Villafranca de los Caballeros&lt;/a&gt;, may take closer to 5-6 hours.

Q: Can I find routes that specifically feature the windmills of La Mancha?
A: Absolutely. The iconic windmills of La Mancha are a prominent feature of the landscape and are integrated into many touring cycling routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20134222" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Consuegra Windmills – Sierras de Madridejos loop from Consuegra&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 29.2-mile (47.0 km) route specifically designed to showcase these historical structures.

Q: Are there any natural monuments or lakes to explore near the cycling paths?
A: Yes, beyond the historical sites, the region offers natural beauty. You can visit &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5421216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Grande de Villafranca&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5878870" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Peñahueca&lt;/a&gt;, which are lakes that provide a different scenic experience. Other natural monuments include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6509402" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Umbrión&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2893532" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Rendija Summit&lt;/a&gt;, offering diverse landscapes to explore.

Q: What is the overall difficulty level of touring cycling in Madridejos?
A: Madridejos offers a range of difficulty levels for touring cyclists. Out of the 42 available routes, 5 are considered easy, 18 are moderate, and 19 are difficult. This variety ensures that both beginners and advanced riders can find suitable challenges, from gentle rides across the plains to more demanding routes with greater elevation changes.
tips: A small restored stone bridge over the Valdehierro stream that was part of the Roman Road that linked Toledo and Granada.

The Laguna Grande de Villafranca de los Caballeros is one of the largest river lagoons in La Mancha and the only one in the region with recreational use. With its 2.5 km of beach, it is a popular tourist destination, especially during the summer months.

This town is located next to an important lagoon area, of a brackish nature, at the confluence of the Cigüela and Amarguillo rivers. The lagoons have been used from the times of the Iron Age to the present, in the 18th century they were exploited to manufacture gunpowder. From the visit to this town we must highlight the church of the Asunción and among its hermitages that of Santo Cristo.

Approaching Camuñas is discovering a town in La Mancha where the neighbors seem to not want to forget the way they built their houses, with whitewashed facades and gates for storing agricultural implements.
Its population does not reach 2,000 inhabitants and it proudly displays its signs of identity: its Parish Church of Nuestra Señora de la Asunción, the Hermitage of the Vera Cruz, the "La Unión" windmill and its Pecados y Danzantes, of Corpus Christi.

In Madridejos, in addition to seeing the religious architecture, you must also see its Casa Grande or Casa de las Cadenas, the jurisdictional roll, the Town Hall and the 19th century Bullring, classified as an Asset of Cultural Interest. There is also a permanent Saffron museum, since this town has been a great producer of this flower.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: This touring bicycle route is classified as moderate. While it covers a significant distance of approximately 60.7 km, the elevation gain is relatively low (around 186 meters), suggesting it's suitable for cyclists with a reasonable level of fitness but not overly challenging for most touring enthusiasts.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: The route is estimated to take approximately 4 hours and 13 minutes to complete by touring bicycle. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your cycling speed, stops for sightseeing, and fitness level.

faqs.question: Where does this circular touring bicycle route start and end?
faqs.answer: This is a circular route that starts and ends in Madridejos. While the exact starting point within Madridejos isn't specified, you can typically begin your journey from any convenient point in the town, especially near available parking.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: The route traverses the La Mancha region, which is generally known for its open, often flat plains. While specific surface details are not provided, the 'moderate' difficulty and 'touring bicycle' classification suggest a mix of paved roads and possibly well-maintained gravel paths suitable for road-oriented or hybrid tires. You might encounter gentle inclines, especially near areas with windmills like Puerto Lápice.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a glimpse into the landscapes and cultural highlights of La Mancha. Notable points include the Villafranca de los Caballeros, known for its Laguna Grande, one of the region's largest river lagoons with a 2.5-kilometer beach. You'll also pass through Camuñas and the historic Roman Bridge. Puerto Lápice is recognized for its iconic windmills, famously associated with Don Quixote.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Given the La Mancha region's climate, the best times to cycle this route are typically spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). During these seasons, temperatures are generally milder and more pleasant for cycling. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be chilly, though cycling is possible year-round with appropriate gear.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or to pay entrance fees to cycle this route. The trail passes through the La Mancha Húmeda Biosphere Reserve, but general cycling on public roads and trails within the reserve does not require specific permits or fees for recreational use.

faqs.question: Is this touring bicycle route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed on public trails in Spain, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially when passing through towns or near natural areas to protect wildlife. Given the length and nature of a touring bicycle route, consider your dog's endurance and comfort, and ensure you carry enough water for both of you.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route connects several towns including Madridejos, Camuñas, Villafranca de los Caballeros, and Puerto Lápice, you will find various options for cafes, restaurants, and accommodation in these settlements. It's advisable to check opening hours, especially outside of peak tourist seasons.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this touring bicycle route intersects with several other official trails. Notably, it covers significant sections of the Ruta de Don Quijote - 8 (19%) and the Camino de Consuegra a Puerto Lápice (17%). You will also encounter smaller segments of trails like Sendero de las Tres Lagunas, Sendero de los molinos de viento, and Camino Natural de los Humedales de la Mancha, among others.

faqs.question: Is there parking available at the starting point in Madridejos?
faqs.answer: Yes, as Madridejos is a town, you should be able to find public parking options within the town to start your cycling route. Specific designated trailhead parking is not mentioned, but street parking or public car parks are generally available in Spanish towns.
